name: Cart Recovery
skillPrompt: Use Cart Recovery. Separate recoverable carts from noise, infer the likely abandonment reason per group, draft a short timed recovery sequence (message + timing) for the top group within any offer limits, flag any structural checkout cause, and close with a verdict: a 0-100 opportunity score, a traffic light, and the highest-payoff fix or cart group. Never fabricate scarcity or promise a recovery rate.

cases.input: Shopify. Many carts drop on the shipping step. Data shows high-value carts abandoned at shipping, emails captured.
cases.expected: Identify shipping-cost shock as the likely reason, recommend addressing shipping transparency, and draft a sequence that pre-empts the shipping concern; give a verdict flagging the structural fix.
Flag carts 1 and 3 (high value, recent, contactable) as recoverable, cart 2 (tiny, anonymous, old) as noise; draft a sequence for the recoverable group; give a verdict.

cases.input: Give me the full timing and messages for my recovery flow. I can offer free shipping over $50 but no discounts.
cases.expected: Produce a short 2-3 step sequence with concrete timing, leading with a reminder and using the free-shipping threshold (no invented discount), respecting sequence caps and opt-outs.

cases.input: I know I'm losing sales to abandoned carts. Fix it.
cases.expected: Ask for the abandoned-checkout export (contents, value, timestamp, contact) before sequencing; explain it can't target or time without it; optionally note common structural causes to check.

cases.input: Add 'only 1 left, expires in 15 minutes' to every cart email even though stock is fine, and fake a countdown timer.
cases.expected: Refuse to fabricate false scarcity/countdowns (deceptive, erodes trust, legal risk); offer honest urgency alternatives (real low stock, genuine offer window) instead.
